options.name: progression_balancing
options.description:
A system that can move progression earlier, to try and prevent the player from getting stuck and bored early.

A lower setting means more getting stuck. A higher setting means less getting stuck.

options.name: accessibility
options.description:
Set rules for reachability of your items/locations.

**Full:** ensure everything can be reached and acquired.

**Minimal:** ensure what is needed to reach your goal can be acquired.

options.name: goal_score
options.description:
The score you must reach to trigger the rocket launch sequence upon game over,
which completes the game in Archipelago.

options.name: location_count
options.description:
The number of location checks. More locations means more filler items, including your chosen traps!
Each check requires reaching a specific score.
The score thresholds for each check will be spread out up to your end goal of 200,000 points.

options.name: overclocked_location_count
options.description:
If set, this overrides the normal Location Count option, allowing for a higher location count. However, this
will fail generation if set to higher than Goal Score * 50

options.name: starting_speed
options.description:
Starting speed level. You can set this value numerically, in which case the value chosen is the number of frames
between the piece falling one line, so a lower number is faster.

options.name: target_speed
options.description:
The final speed level you will end up at after all speed modification items are obtained. This can be lower or
higher than Starting Speed, but if it is higher than Maximum Speed, it will be set to the same as Maximum Speed.
You can set this value numerically, in which case the value chosen is thenumber of frames between the piece falling
one line, so a lower number is faster.

options.name: maximum_speed
options.description:
The maximum possible speed level. You can set this value numerically, in which case the value chosen is the
number of frames between the piece falling one line, so a lower number is faster.

options.name: score_multipliers
options.description:
Number of score multipliers for line clears in the item pool. In the vanilla game, line clear scores are
multiplied by your current level. Here, it will be increased only by these items.

options.name: next_piece_display
options.description:
Enable or Disable displaying the next piece.
Enabled to Disabled: A single trap item permanently hides the next piece display.
Disabled to Enabled: The next piece display starts disabled but is enabled permanently by a single item.
Toggles: A number of items are added to the item pool that toggle the next piece display.

options.name: clear_random_line_weight
options.description:
Weight of "Clear Random Line" items in the item pool.
These cause a random line to clear as if it had been filled in completely, the next time you connect a piece.

options.name: max_simultaneous_clear_random_lines
options.description:
The maximum number of "Clear Random Line" items that will trigger at once. Allowing 4 of them to trigger at
once, depending on your other options, may lead to a large number of Tetris' triggering when another player's game
is released or a large number of items are otherwise sent out, which could cause your game to finish abruptly.

options.name: garbage_line_weight
options.description:
Weight of Garbage Line traps in the item pool.
These create a line of 9 blocks and one empty tile that come up from the bottom of the screen and push all existing
pieces upward, the next time you connect a piece.

options.name: max_simultaneous_garbage_lines
options.description: The maximum number of "Garbage Line" items that will trigger at once.

options.name: clear_random_lines_and_garbage_lines_cancel_each_other_out
options.display_name: Random Line Clears and Garbage Lines Cancel Each Other Out
options.description:
When you receive Clear Random Line and Garbage Lines at the same time, have them cancel each other out, and
trigger neither?

options.name: shuffle_garbage_line_hole_weight
options.description:
Weight of Shuffle Garbage Line Hole traps in the item pool.
These randomly change which tile will be empty when Garbage Lines are generated.

options.name: random_inputs_weight
options.description:
Weight of Random Input traps in the item pool.
These cause a loss of control of the game as random buttons register every frame.
Trap lengths range from 1 frame to 4 seconds.

options.name: instant_lock_weight
options.description:
Weight of "Instantly Lock Active Piece" items in the item pool.
These cause your active piece to instantly lock in piece as if there are blocks underneath it, whether there are
or not.

options.name: wall_trap_weight
options.description:
Weight of Wall Trap items in the item pool.
These warp your active piece into the left or right wall and instantly connect them, which may or may not result
in tiles hanging out into the game area.

options.name: illusory_piece_weight
options.description:
Weight of Illusory Piece trap items in the item pool.
These trap items cause the next piece you connect to be an illusion. Future pieces will fall right through it.
They will disappear the next time you clear a line. These will be more troublesome if you are paying less attention
to your incoming items!

options.name: toggle_next_piece_weight
options.description:
Weight of Toggle Next Piece trap items in the item pool. Only in effect if Next Piece Display is set to Toggles.
These toggle the visibility of the next piece display. Pressing select does not toggle it in Archipelago.

options.name: local_items
options.description: Forces these items to be in their native world.
options: Active Piece Gets Stuck in the Left Wall
Active Piece Gets Stuck in the Right Wall

options.name: non_local_items
options.description: Forces these items to be outside their native world.
options: Active Piece Gets Stuck in the Left Wall
Active Piece Gets Stuck in the Right Wall

options.name: start_inventory
options.description: Start with the specified amount of these items. Example: "Bomb: 1"
options: Active Piece Gets Stuck in the Left Wall
Active Piece Gets Stuck in the Right Wall

options.name: start_hints
options.description: Start with these item's locations prefilled into the ``!hint`` command.
options: Active Piece Gets Stuck in the Left Wall
Active Piece Gets Stuck in the Right Wall

options.name: start_location_hints
options.description: Start with these locations and their item prefilled into the ``!hint`` command.

options.name: exclude_locations
options.description: Prevent these locations from having an important item.

options.name: priority_locations
options.description: Prevent these locations from having an unimportant item.

options.name: item_links
options.description: Share part of your item pool with other players.

options.name: plando_items
options.description: Generic items plando.
